levels.name: Mastering Fundamentals
levels.completionRequirement: Complete all 4 required projects and all enhancement tasks

enhancement.notes: Complete Table Topics Speaker before delivering your Ice Breaker speech

projects.name: Ice Breaker
projects.purpose: Introduce yourself to the club and learn the basic structure of a public speech.

projects.name: Writing a Speech with Purpose
projects.purpose: Learn or review basic methods for writing a speech and present a well-organized speech on any topic.

projects.name: Introduction to Vocal Variety and Body Language
projects.purpose: Practice using vocal variety or body language to enhance a speech.

projects.name: Evaluation and Feedback
projects.purpose: Present a speech on any topic, receive feedback, and apply the feedback to a second speech.

levels.name: Learning Your Style
levels.completionRequirement: Complete all 3 required projects and all enhancement tasks

projects.name: Understanding Your Leadership Style
projects.purpose: Identify your primary leadership style or styles and understand how it impacts the people around you.

projects.name: Understanding Your Communication Style
projects.purpose: Learn about different communication styles, identify your primary style, and understand its impact on your interactions.

projects.name: Introduction to Toastmasters Mentoring
projects.purpose: Clearly define how Toastmasters envisions mentoring by sharing a time when you were a protégé.

levels.name: Increasing Knowledge
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 1 required project, 2 elective projects, and all enhancement tasks

projects.name: Negotiate the Best Outcome
projects.purpose: Learn about different types of negotiation and the strategies that can be used when negotiating.

projects.name: Deliver Social Speeches
projects.purpose: Practice delivering two different social speeches — such as a toast, eulogy, or tribute — in front of your club.

projects.name: Using Presentation Software
projects.purpose: Introduce or review basic presentation software strategies for creating and using slides to support or enhance a speech.

projects.name: Connect with Storytelling
projects.purpose: Practice using a story within a speech or giving a speech that is a story.

projects.name: Creating Effective Visual Aids
projects.purpose: Practice selecting and using a variety of visual aids during a speech.

projects.name: Using Descriptive Language
projects.purpose: Practice writing a speech with an emphasis on adding language to increase interest and impact.

projects.name: Connect with Your Audience
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to connect with an unfamiliar audience by selecting a topic new to your club members.

projects.name: Make Connections Through Networking
projects.purpose: Develop and practice a personal strategy for building connections through networking.

projects.name: Focus on the Positive
projects.purpose: Practice being aware of your thoughts and feelings, as well as the impact of your responses on others.

projects.name: Inspire Your Audience
projects.purpose: Practice writing and delivering a speech that inspires others.

projects.name: Prepare for an Interview
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to present yourself well in an interview through a role-play presentation.

projects.name: Understanding Vocal Variety
projects.purpose: Practice using vocal variety — including pitch, rate, volume, and pauses — to enhance a speech.

projects.name: Effective Body Language
projects.purpose: Deliver a speech with awareness of your intentional and unintentional body language to reinforce your verbal message.

projects.name: Active Listening
projects.purpose: Demonstrate your ability to listen to what others say by serving as Topicsmaster at a club meeting.

projects.name: Know Your Sense of Humor
projects.purpose: Begin developing a collection of humorous stories and present a speech that includes at least one humorous anecdote.

projects.name: Researching and Presenting
projects.purpose: Learn or review basic research methods and present a well-organized, well-researched speech on any topic.

levels.name: Building Skills
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 1 required project, 1 elective project, and all enhancement tasks

roles: Club Officer or Committee Chair activities

projects.name: Manage Change
projects.purpose: Practice developing a change management plan and share it with your club.

projects.name: Create a Podcast
projects.purpose: Introduce yourself to the skills needed to organize and present a podcast by recording at least 60 minutes of content.

projects.name: Building a Social Media Presence
projects.purpose: Apply your understanding of social media to build or enhance an established social media presence.

projects.name: Managing a Difficult Audience
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to address audience challenges when you present outside of your Toastmasters club.

projects.name: Write a Compelling Blog
projects.purpose: Review or introduce the skills needed to write and maintain a blog by posting at least eight times in one month.

projects.name: Manage Online Meetings
projects.purpose: Practice facilitating an online meeting or leading a webinar of 20 to 25 minutes for fellow Toastmasters.

projects.name: Question-and-Answer Session
projects.purpose: Learn about and practice facilitating a question-and-answer session following a prepared speech.

projects.name: Public Relations Strategies
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to effectively use public relations strategies for any group or situation.

projects.name: Manage Projects Successfully
projects.purpose: Practice developing a plan, building a team, and fulfilling the plan with the help of your team.

levels.name: Demonstrating Expertise
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 1 required project, 1 elective project, and all enhancement tasks

roles: Serve in a club leadership role for at least 6 months

projects.name: Lead in Any Situation
projects.purpose: Apply the skills needed to successfully lead in a volunteer or other organization for at least six months.

projects.name: Lessons Learned
projects.purpose: Learn about and apply the skills needed to run a lessons learned meeting during or after a project.

projects.name: Moderate a Panel Discussion
projects.purpose: Apply your skills as a public speaker and leader to plan and facilitate a panel discussion.

projects.name: Ethical Leadership
projects.purpose: Develop a clear understanding of your ethical framework and moderate a panel discussion about ethics.

projects.name: Leading in Your Volunteer Organization
projects.purpose: Apply the skills needed to lead in a volunteer organization for at least six months and reflect on the experience.

projects.name: Prepare to Speak Professionally
projects.purpose: Practice developing and presenting a longer keynote-style speech suitable for a professional audience.

projects.name: High Performance Leadership
projects.purpose: Apply your leadership and planning knowledge to develop a project plan, organize a guidance committee, and implement your plan.

projects.purpose: Develop your method for giving impromptu speeches and practice adapting rehearsed stories during a presentation.

projects.name: Focus on the Positive
projects.purpose: Practice being aware of your thoughts and feelings, as well as the impact of your responses on others.

projects.name: Inspire Your Audience
projects.purpose: Practice writing and delivering a speech that inspires others.

projects.name: Prepare for an Interview
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to present yourself well in an interview through a role-play presentation.

projects.name: Understanding Vocal Variety
projects.purpose: Practice using vocal variety — including pitch, rate, volume, and pauses — to enhance a speech.

projects.name: Effective Body Language
projects.purpose: Deliver a speech with awareness of your intentional and unintentional body language to reinforce your verbal message.

projects.name: Active Listening
projects.purpose: Demonstrate your ability to listen to what others say by serving as Topicsmaster at a club meeting.

projects.name: Know Your Sense of Humor
projects.purpose: Begin developing a collection of humorous stories and present a speech that includes at least one humorous anecdote.

projects.name: Researching and Presenting
projects.purpose: Learn or review basic research methods and present a well-organized, well-researched speech on any topic.

levels.name: Building Skills
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 1 required project, 1 elective project, and all enhancement tasks

roles: Club Officer or Committee Chair activities

projects.name: Communicate Change
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to effectively communicate change to a group or organization.

projects.name: Create a Podcast
projects.purpose: Introduce yourself to the skills needed to organize and present a podcast by recording at least 60 minutes of content.

projects.name: Building a Social Media Presence
projects.purpose: Apply your understanding of social media to build or enhance an established social media presence.

projects.name: Managing a Difficult Audience
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to address audience challenges when you present outside of your Toastmasters club.

projects.name: Write a Compelling Blog
projects.purpose: Review or introduce the skills needed to write and maintain a blog by posting at least eight times in one month.

projects.name: Manage Online Meetings
projects.purpose: Practice facilitating an online meeting or leading a webinar of 20 to 25 minutes for fellow Toastmasters.

projects.name: Question-and-Answer Session
projects.purpose: Learn about and practice facilitating a question-and-answer session following a prepared speech.

projects.name: Public Relations Strategies
projects.purpose: Practice the skills needed to effectively use public relations strategies for any group or situation.

projects.name: Manage Projects Successfully
projects.purpose: Practice developing a plan, building a team, and fulfilling the plan with the help of your team.

levels.name: Demonstrating Expertise
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 1 required project, 1 elective project, and all enhancement tasks

roles: Serve in a club leadership role for at least 6 months

projects.name: Develop Your Vision
projects.purpose: Develop a detailed vision for your personal life, professional life, or an organization, and establish a plan to implement it.

projects.name: Lessons Learned
projects.purpose: Learn about and apply the skills needed to run a lessons learned meeting during or after a project.

projects.name: Moderate a Panel Discussion
projects.purpose: Apply your skills as a public speaker and leader to plan and facilitate a panel discussion.

projects.name: Ethical Leadership
projects.purpose: Develop a clear understanding of your ethical framework and moderate a panel discussion about ethics.

projects.name: High Performance Leadership
projects.purpose: Apply your leadership and planning knowledge to develop a project plan, organize a guidance committee, and implement your plan.

projects.name: Leading in Your Volunteer Organization
projects.purpose: Apply the skills needed to lead in a volunteer organization for at least six months and reflect on the experience.

projects.name: Prepare to Speak Professionally
projects.purpose: Practice developing and presenting a longer keynote-style speech suitable for a professional audience.

levels.name: Introduction to Basic Training
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 2 required speeches and 1 role assignment

projects.name: The Ice Breaker
projects.purpose: Introduce yourself to the club and begin your Toastmasters journey with a short speech about yourself.

projects.name: Be in Earnest
projects.purpose: Deliver a dynamic appeal on a topic you feel passionate about to help overcome nervousness and connect emotionally with your audience.

projects.name: When You Are Table Topic Chairperson
projects.purpose: Serve as Table Topics Chairperson at a club meeting, leading impromptu speaking exercises for fellow members.

levels.name: Developing Your Skills
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 1 role assignment and 2 required speeches

projects.name: When You Are Toastmaster of the Evening
projects.purpose: Serve as Toastmaster of the Evening, learning to plan and orchestrate a complete club meeting from start to finish.

projects.name: Building a Speech
projects.purpose: Learn the fundamental structure of a well-built speech by organizing your ideas into a clear beginning, middle, and end.

projects.name: Hands Up
projects.purpose: Develop confidence in using gesture and physical movement to reinforce and add impact to your spoken message.

levels.name: Building on the Fundamentals
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 2 required speeches, 1 role assignment, and 1 self-evaluation

projects.name: Vocal Variety
projects.purpose: Practice using pitch, rate, volume, and pauses to make your speech more expressive, engaging, and effective.

projects.name: Speech Building
projects.purpose: Demonstrate your growing ability to organize and develop a coherent, well-supported speech on a topic of your choice.

projects.name: When You Are a Critic or Evaluator
projects.purpose: Serve as a speech evaluator at a club meeting, providing constructive, encouraging feedback to a fellow member.

projects.name: Take Your Measure Again
projects.purpose: Complete a structured self-evaluation to reflect on your growth as a speaker and identify areas for continued improvement.

levels.name: Advancing Your Technique
levels.completionRequirement: Complete all 3 required speeches

projects.name: Reading the Speech
projects.purpose: Learn the special skill of reading aloud effectively — with natural expression, meaningful pauses, and consistent eye contact.

projects.name: Illuminate the Speech
projects.purpose: Use examples, comparisons, and analogies to clarify your ideas and make abstract concepts vivid and memorable.

projects.name: Working With Words
projects.purpose: Build your vocabulary and develop the skill of choosing words that precisely and powerfully communicate your thoughts.

levels.name: Mastering the Craft
levels.completionRequirement: Complete all 3 required speeches

projects.name: Special Types of Speech
projects.purpose: Explore and practice a special speech format — such as a eulogy, tribute, toast, or nomination — for a specific occasion.

projects.name: Organizing the Speech
projects.purpose: Review and apply advanced principles of speech organization to craft a presentation that flows logically and stays memorable.

projects.name: Carrying On
projects.purpose: Deliver a capstone speech that showcases the communication skills you have developed throughout the Basic Training path.

levels.name: Introduction to Competent Communication
levels.completionRequirement: Complete all 3 required projects

projects.name: The Ice Breaker
projects.purpose: Introduce yourself to the club with a prepared speech on any topic, establishing a foundation for your communication journey.

projects.name: Organize Your Speech
projects.purpose: Learn to present a well-organized speech with a clear beginning, middle, and end.

projects.name: Get to the Point
projects.purpose: Practice presenting your ideas concisely and organizing your speech around a single, clear objective.

levels.name: Building Core Skills
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 3 required speeches and 1 self-evaluation

projects.name: How to Say It
projects.purpose: Select the right words and sentence structure to clearly and vividly communicate your ideas.

projects.name: Your Body Speaks
projects.purpose: Use stance, gestures, eye contact, and movement to reinforce and add meaning to your words.

projects.name: Evaluate Your Progress, Part 1
projects.purpose: Assess your speaking skills at the midpoint of the Competent Communication program and identify areas for growth.

projects.name: Vocal Variety
projects.purpose: Use vocal pitch, tone, rate, and volume as tools to enhance your delivery and strengthen your message.

levels.name: Advanced Fundamentals
levels.completionRequirement: Complete 4 required speeches and 1 self-evaluation

projects.name: Research Your Topic
projects.purpose: Collect information about a topic from numerous sources, then carefully support your assertions with that information.

projects.name: Get Comfortable with Visual Aids
projects.purpose: Select, prepare, and use visual aids that clearly support your speech and are appropriate to your topic.

projects.name: Persuade with Power
projects.purpose: Persuade your audience through the effective use of logic, emotion, and your credibility as a speaker.

projects.name: Inspire Your Audience
projects.purpose: Inspire your audience with a longer speech that applies a variety of rhetorical and motivational techniques.

projects.name: Evaluate Your Progress, Part 2
projects.purpose: Assess your overall growth as a speaker after completing the Competent Communication program.

levels.name: The Entertaining Speaker
levels.completionRequirement: Complete all 5 required speeches

projects.name: The Entertaining Speech
projects.purpose: Prepare and deliver an entertaining speech that captivates your audience — making them laugh, think, or feel deeply moved.

projects.name: Resources for Entertainment
projects.purpose: Identify and practice using the tools of entertainment: humor, dramatic story, audience involvement, and personal style.

projects.name: Make Them Laugh
projects.purpose: Use humor effectively in a speech by applying techniques such as puns, self-deprecation, exaggeration, and comic timing.

projects.name: A Dramatic Talk
projects.purpose: Captivate your audience by using dramatic vocal and physical techniques to tell a compelling and vivid story.

projects.name: Speaking After Dinner
projects.purpose: Deliver an entertaining after-dinner speech that sets a warm, humorous, and celebratory tone appropriate for a social occasion.

levels.name: Storytelling
levels.completionRequirement: Complete all 5 required speeches

projects.name: The Folk Tale
projects.purpose: Tell a traditional folk tale or fairy tale, adapting it to suit your personal style and engage your audience.

projects.name: Let's Get Personal
projects.purpose: Tell a personal story from your own life that reveals something meaningful about who you are.

projects.name: The Moral of the Story
projects.purpose: Tell a story with a clear moral or lesson, using narrative to communicate a meaningful message.

projects.name: The Touching Story
projects.purpose: Tell an emotionally resonant story that moves your audience and leaves a lasting impression.

projects.name: Bringing History to Life
projects.purpose: Research a historical figure or event and present it as an engaging, vivid, and humanizing narrative.
